refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting achievement of a productivity target defined by the customer, measured in specific units. It is solution-neutral and specifies what the system must do without implying how. The units 'm³/h' or 'ton/h' are part of the measurement criteria, not implementation details. Therefore, the FR is atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational testing and data collection during representative operations that the NGHE solution enables achieving or exceeding the customer-defined productivity target (in m³/h or ton/h) over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Adjust processing rate dynamically based on real-time throughput feedback
Allocate resources to maintain or exceed target productivity
Optimize task sequencing to maximize output within cycle time
Monitor and control operational parameters to sustain productivity levels
selected_dp.selected_dp: Adjust processing rate dynamically based on real-time throughput feedback

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a feedback control approach that can be modularized independently from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely measurement of productivity is available
System can adjust processing rate without violating safety or operational policies
External factors affecting productivity can be sensed or compensated
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including measurement and feedback mechanisms in the FR to clarify the need for real-time productivity monitoring and control.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing logic and system-level control functions, including dynamic adjustment of processing rates based on real-time throughput feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time throughput data and system status information for feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends adjusted processing rate commands to downstream modules or components.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems and components for data exchange and control signals.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the dynamic processing adjustment is a core system function; minimal diffusion as most related data and control remain internal.
Partial containment possible but would require significant data exchange with other modules, increasing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than internal processing adjustments.
Very low containment; safety services are orthogonal to processing rate adjustments.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP 'Adjust processing rate dynamically based on real-time throughput feedback' because it centralizes processing logic and feedback handling, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth within NGHE Solution if too many functions are centralized, risking reduced modularity.
Dependency on accurate and timely telemetry data may cause coupling with external systems, impacting module independence.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary and maintains a good balance between specificity and containment.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Adjust processing rate dynamically based on real-time throughput feedback' directly addresses the FR of supporting the customer-defined productivity target by enabling real-time control of processing rate to meet volume or mass per hour goals. This mapping is logically sound and grounded in the acceptance criteria of achieving productivity targets during operations.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on telemetry data from external systems may introduce coupling, but this is managed within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the DP, with minimal diffusion risk as most related data and control remain internal. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring productivity metrics during representative operations and validating that the NGHE Solution module adjusts processing rates accordingly to meet targets.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces for telemetry input and tasking output supporting the implementation.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary and maintains a good balance between specificity and containment.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no recommended revisions. The DP 'Adjust processing rate dynamically based on real-time throughput feedback' fully satisfies the FR of supporting customer-defined productivity targets, and the NGHE Solution module is an appropriate implementation boundary with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and no further decomposition is necessary at this stage given the high safety criticality and the current well-defined mapping.

assumptions: Telemetry data from external systems is reliable and timely.
The NGHE Solution module can effectively control processing rate in real time.
open_risks: Dependency on external telemetry data introduces coupling risk, though managed within NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Operational data demonstrating the NGHE Solution module's ability to meet productivity targets under varying conditions.
Verification test results confirming real-time adjustment effectiveness and safety compliance.
